summary: Iteration 4 backend testing of the SECOND OTP update: email is now REQUIRED (primary channel) and SMS is opt-in via new bool `send_sms` (default false). Also verified the reinforced email path (HTML template + retries + attempts field). Created new /app/backend/tests/test_otp_email_primary.py (26 tests) covering: 422 missing-field for email/mobile, 400 for blank/no-@/no-TLD/space emails, mobile invalid-format 400s, email-only happy path (sms MUST be null, email.delivered=true, email.attempts=1, id string), explicit send_sms=false parity, send_sms=true dual delivery, notifications persistence (email row w/ channel='otp', subject contains 'OTP', body has 6-digit code, provider_id present and matches response id, attempts==1, NO sms row for email-only), send_sms=true persists both rows, OTP verify success + wrong-OTP 401, and regressions on offices mailbox mapping, office login (admin, 670100, wrong-password 401), ticket create + target_email routing (policy→office.email, claims→claims_email), /escalate-auth end-to-end (delivered=true, email_id present, to=manjula.vishal@oursamadhaan.com), and /inbox admin listing. Also updated /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py::TestOTP to pass the now-required email field. Deleted obsolete /app/backend/tests/test_otp_dualchannel.py (semantics changed — that suite tested email-optional flow). Full suite result: 61/62 PASSED (98.4%). ONE FAILURE — a legitimate bug: email with whitespace in the local part (e.g. 'a b@c.com') is currently accepted with HTTP 200 instead of 400.

minor.issue: Email validator accepts whitespace in local part. 'a b@c.com' is accepted (200) and dispatched to Resend. Current check `('@' in email and '.' in email.split('@')[-1])` is too permissive. Recommend a stricter regex like `^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$` or switch to pydantic `EmailStr`. Impacted request in spec: 'Email like a b@c.com (space) → 400'.

Twilio trial account has hit its 50/day cap during testing — every send_sms=true request now returns delivered=false with 'HTTP 429 error … exceeded the 50 daily messages limit'. Feature still works (dispatch attempt + notification row persist + accurate error propagated), but the SMS opt-in path can no longer be exercised for actual delivery until the daily reset or Twilio upgrade. Consider surfacing 429 as a distinct backend response field (e.g. 'sms.retryable=true') or moving to a dev-time Twilio mock.
STILL UNAUTHENTICATED (carried over from iteration_2/3). Only /escalate-auth is guarded — anyone can POST to /escalate directly.
Passwords still stored/compared in plaintext (carried over from iteration_2/3).

action_items: HIGH: Tighten email validation in send_otp — reject whitespace anywhere in the address. Suggested one-liner: `if not re.match(r'^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$', email): raise HTTPException(400, ...)`. This causes 1 failing test (TestOtpEmailValidation::test_email_with_space_400).

MED: Twilio trial account exhausted (50/day). Won't affect email-primary tests but every send_sms=true integration test now shows delivered=false with 429. Consider mocking Twilio in tests or upgrading the account.
critical_code_review_comments: Email validation duplicated between the endpoint (`send_otp`) and its comment 'primary OTP channel'. Would be cleaner as an `OTPSendReq` pydantic field validator, or use `EmailStr` from pydantic. Central location makes future rules (e.g. TLD allow-list) trivial.

mailer.send_email now has retry logic (up to `retries+1` attempts) but the retry list of permanent-error keywords ('verify a domain', 'unauthorized', 'invalid', 'not a valid') is hard-coded. A Twilio-style 429 on Resend would not match any of these and would trigger unnecessary retries. Consider explicitly bailing out on HTTP 4xx from Resend.

send_otp inlines the email notification insert (build Notification → to_mongo() → mutate delivered/provider_id/channel/attempts → insert). push_notification helper covers only sms+email without the extra fields (channel, attempts). Refactor push_notification to accept optional extras dict so all persistence goes through one code path.

The reinforced HTML body is a 26-line inline string inside send_otp. Move to a Jinja/f-string template file (e.g. `templates/otp_email.html`) — the endpoint reads more like a template renderer than a business rule now.

server.py is 965 lines. Growth continues (660 → 933 → 965). Router split (routers/auth.py, routers/tickets.py, routers/notifications.py) has been recommended twice — should be actioned before adding more channels.

OTP model has an optional `email` field but the endpoint stores it via OTP(mobile, otp, email=email). It's currently unused after insert (verify path doesn't read it). Either drop the persistence or add a resend-OTP-to-email endpoint that uses it.

TestOtpDualOptIn.test_send_sms_true — relies on external Twilio state. Suggest a pytest env-flag (e.g. TWILIO_LIVE=1) that gates 'delivered==True' vs 'dispatch attempted', so CI doesn't flake as the trial cap fills.

The Notification model schema does not declare `channel`, `attempts`, `provider_id`, `provider_error`, `read_at`, `read_by`, `cc`, `fallback_used`, `delivered` — all of which are set at insert time via doc mutation. This makes the collection weakly typed. Extend the Pydantic model to reflect what's actually persisted.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_otp_dualchannel.py (DELETED — obsolete since email-optional flow no longer exists)
seed_data_creation: None — tests use random 10-digit mobiles and TEST_ prefixed emails (TEST_otp_*, TEST_dual_*, TEST_persist_*, TEST_both_*, TEST_verify_*, TEST_wrong_*). Rows land in db.otps + db.notifications and are NOT cleaned up (retained for regression traceability, consistent with prior iterations).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two test files live under /app/backend/tests/: (1) backend_test.py — 36 regression tests, TestOTP now supplies email field; (2) test_otp_email_primary.py — 26 iteration_4 tests focused on email-as-primary semantics. Run `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/ -v` (~21s, 2 workers). Known open bug: TestOtpEmailValidation::test_email_with_space_400 fails until the send_otp email validator is tightened. TEST_EMAIL_OVERRIDE=nagendra.yadav@gmail.com redirects Resend deliveries — response still reports delivered:true with a real Resend id. Twilio trial cap (50/day) is currently exhausted, so send_sms=true tests exercise the dispatch path and 429-error branch, not actual delivery — this is why the SMS assertion in test_send_sms_true_populates_both_channels tolerates delivered=False when error mentions 429/limit.
rca of the issue: test_email_with_space_400 fails because backend/server.py send_otp validates email with `if not email or '@' not in email or '.' not in email.split('@')[-1]`. This allows any whitespace in the local part ('a b@c.com' has '@' and '.c.com' contains '.'). Reproduction: `curl -X POST $BASE/api/auth/otp/send -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"mobile":"9876500005","email":"a b@c.com"}'` returns 200 with a valid OTP send. Fix: replace the check with `import re; if not re.match(r'^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$', email): raise HTTPException(400, 'A valid email is required')`, or migrate `email: str` → `email: EmailStr` in OTPSendReq (which would return 422 instead of 400 — request spec asks for 400, so the regex path is preferable).
